title: Proof Boundary — The No Idle State Rule
supported_claims: The No Idle State Rule is codified in the repository governance and Master State operating rules.
The runtime uses explicit state, queues, gates, events, and fallback adapters to continue unblocked work.
The rule distinguishes valid stop conditions from artificial stoppage.
unsupported_claims: Continuous execution eliminates all errors or judgment failures.
Every external dependency can always be worked around.
The rule has measured productivity outcomes outside this implementation.
